revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:Á•ěÁ§ĺŚļĀ]]
'''JinjachŇć''' (Á•ěÁ§ĺŚļĀ) is the local branch of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]] established one per each {{ill|Prefectures of Japan|en|Prefectures of Japan|qid=Q50337|lt=Prefecture|1=Prefectures of Japan}}. Although it is called a "chŇć" (ŚļĀ), it is not a government office, but rather an umbrella religious corporation under the jurisdiction of the {{ill|Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|en|Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|qid=Q842359|lt=Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology|1=Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology}} based on the {{ill|Religious Corporations Act|ja|Śģóśēôś≥ēšļļś≥ē|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Corporations Act|1=Religious Corporation Act|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11451965}}.<ref>[https://www.bunka.go.jp/tokei_hakusho_shuppan/hakusho_nenjihokokusho/shukyo_nenkan/pdf/h30nenkan.pdf]{{ill|Religious Yearbook, Heisei 30 Edition|ja|ŚģóśēôŚĻīťĎĎ ŚĻ≥śąź30th year ÁČą|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Religious Yearbook, Heisei 30 Edition|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|qid=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} (ed.) p.103</ref>
Association of Shinto Shrines is responsible for handling various administrative matters‚ÄĒincluding personnel and finances of prefectural {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}}‚ÄĒguiding shrines and [[:en:Kannushi|Shinto priests]], and promoting ritual and community activities. It also serves as the public relations office for shrine activities. In most cases, the office of the Association of Shinto Shrines is located within or adjacent to a relatively large shrine in that prefecture.
As a subordinate organization of the Association of Shinto Shrines, regional branches are established. These branches divide each prefecture into several districts, with one branch set up per district.
Many Association of Shinto Shrines have been incorporated as {{ill|Religious corporation|lt=Religious corporation|lt=Religious corporations|ja|Śģóśēôś≥ēšļļ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/religious corporations|qq=}}, in which case they become subordinate corporations of the Association of Shinto Shrines. Even then, the shrines within that prefecture are not managed by the local Association of Shinto Shrines office but are directly administered by the subordinate corporation of the Association of Shinto Shrines.
